title: The Memory Problem, Baseten | Compile 26
summary: Baseten's research team discusses techniques for addressing the memory problem in long-horizon agents, focusing on KV cache compression and iterative compaction to achieve near-lossless retrieval of relevant context at inference time. The work explores selection versus synthesis approaches and amortization using sparse autoencoders.
writeup: Baseten's research team examines the memory problem in long-horizon agents: retaining relevant context as interactions extend without overwhelming the model. The talk centers on KV cache compression and iterative compaction aimed at near-lossless retrieval of relevant context at inference time. It contrasts selection versus synthesis approaches and covers amortization using sparse autoencoders, situating the work alongside sparse attention and test-time compute.
